data_caveats.currency_mixing_corruption: Bundle financials block is in TWD billions; price is USD. enterprise_value_b ($15,420B), net_debt_b (-$1,703B), ev_ebitda_ttm (5.4x), ev_sales_ttm (3.76x), pb (66.1x) are CORRUPTED by mixing the TWD balance sheet with the USD price. DO NOT USE. market_cap_b $2,241B is approximately correct (USD). shares_outstanding 5.186B is the ADR-equivalent count.

twd_to_usd_translation.note: Translated at ~31.58 TWD/USD. Company reports FY2025 USD revenue at ~$118-120B; our 120.6 is consistent.

data_caveats.usable_usd_adr_fields: price 432.15, pe_ttm 37.2x, pe_ntm 22.0x, eps_ntm 19.66, target_mean 473.40, target_high 700, target_low 354, 18 analysts, rec strong_buy (5 strongBuy/12 buy/2 hold/0 sell) are USD/ADR and usable, subject to the eps_ntm forward-skew flag below.

estimate_artifact_check.verdict: FORWARD-SKEWED, NOT STRICTLY NEXT-TWELVE-MONTHS. Q1 2026 actual EPS was US$3.49/ADR (pr.tsmc.com news 3297, 2026-04-16). Q2 2026 guided revenue $39.0-40.2B (+10% q/q) at 65.5-67.5% GM implies ~$3.8-4.0/ADR. FY2026 ADR EPS therefore tracks ~$15-16. The $19.66 figure (~$4.9/qtr) sits above the current run-rate and is best read as an FY2027 / blended +1y consensus, not a clean NTM number.

corrected_framing.fy2026e_eps_usd_adr_approx: 15.5 to 16.0 (run-rate from Q1 actual + Q2 guide + >30% USD revenue growth)
corrected_framing.fy2027e_eps_usd_adr_approx: ~18 to 20 (consistent with the bundle eps_ntm 19.66 as a +1y forward)
corrected_framing.pe_on_fy2026e: 432.15 / ~15.75 = ~27x (this is the cleaner forward P/E; corroborated by Zacks citing 25.0x forward on a slightly different EPS base)

estimate_artifact_check.instruction_to_run: Value on the corrected ladder: ~27x on FY2026e EPS, ~22x on FY2027e. The 22x NTM headline understates the multiple on the nearest fiscal year. Do not treat 22x as the multiple on current earnings.

consensus_snapshot.source: yfinance via Hermes bundle (2026-06-18); corroborated by 24/7 Wall St citing a ~$477 mean target late Apr 2026

whats_priced_in.reverse_multiple_intuition: At $432 the ADR carries ~22x the +1y (FY2027) consensus EPS and ~27x the FY2026e run-rate. Stripping a ~3% terminal growth and an ~11-12% equity cost, a 22x exit-style multiple is consistent with the market underwriting high-20s%/year earnings growth sustained for roughly 4-5 years before fading toward a mature high-teens multiple. That is precisely the AI/HPC durability the company itself guides to: >30% USD revenue growth in 2026 and a 54-56% AI-accelerator revenue CAGR through 2029 (Q1 2026 call). The street is NOT pricing peak-cycle mean-reversion; it is pricing multi-year leading-edge scarcity.
whats_priced_in.what_the_mean_target_implies: The $473 mean target (~+9.5% over 12m) prices continued >30% 2026 growth and a stable-to-rising leading-edge mix, with the multiple roughly held. The $700 high target underwrites the AI-CAGR persisting and 2nm/A16 pricing power lifting gross margin back above 60% structurally; the $354 low target prices an AI-capex digestion and capacity-underutilization scenario.
whats_priced_in.key_pattern: TSM is priced as the structurally-scarce toll on AI compute, not as a cyclical foundry. The risk is therefore EXPECTATIONS/VALUATION (priced-for-continuation), not visible fundamental deterioration — monthly sales and the order book are still accelerating.

street_view: TSM is the indispensable leading-edge foundry for the AI buildout; supply is tight through 2027, leading-edge (7nm and below) is 74% of wafer revenue and HPC is 61% of total, and management guides >30% USD revenue growth in 2026 with a 54-56% AI-accelerator CAGR to 2029 -- the debate is valuation/duration, not whether demand is real.

post_earnings_analyst_actions.note: Set a new street-high ADR target after Q1 2026; AI/HPC-driven; one of the most bullish on the tape.
Cited strong HPC/AI chip demand. NT$2,530 ~ US$400/ADR at 31.58 TWD/USD x5 / ADR -- below current price, reflecting a TWD-target lag vs the ADR run.
Flagged Q2 revenue guide of +10% q/q as ahead of the 5-10% consensus expectation.
Forecasts ~30% USD revenue growth in 2026. The early-2026 NT$2,100 target (~US$333/ADR) is now stale vs the $432 ADR run.
Multi-year AI growth engine thesis; drove a record-high rally in early 2026. NT$ target now lags the ADR.
Post-Q1 mean ADR target; 18 analysts; net upgrade momentum with no sell ratings.

analyst_tape_note: The tape is a wall of RAISES with zero sell ratings. Caveat: the Taiwan-listed (NT$) targets from several global banks were set in Jan-Apr 2026 and now translate BELOW the $432 ADR (the ADR has run faster than the TWD-line targets were refreshed). The current USD/ADR-coverage mean is $473 with a $700 street high (Aletheia $600 is the highest verified single-name). This is a bullish but not unanimous-conviction picture: 2 holds and a visible bubble/valuation debate persist.
news_narrative: The narrative is unambiguously demand-led and supply-constrained. (1) Q1 2026 (reported 2026-04-16) was a clean BEAT AND RAISE, not a beat-but-no-raise: revenue US$35.90B (+40.6% y/y, +6.4% q/q) beat the ~$35.5B consensus, EPS NT$22.08 / US$3.49 ADR beat NT$20.88, gross margin 66.2% (+3.9pp q/q), and management RAISED full-year 2026 USD revenue growth guidance to above 30% (pr.tsmc.com news 3297; Quartr Q1 2026 call). (2) Capex guidance was lifted to the HIGH END of the $52-56B range, signalling confidence, not retrenchment. (3) Recent headlines ('AMD, Intel Rise as TSMC Capacity Tightens', GuruFocus 2026-06-17) confirm leading-edge capacity is sold out, giving TSM pricing power. (4) The counter-narrative is a valuation/bubble debate ('TSM at $426: Bubble Territory or a Buy', Zacks 2026-06-17) -- which resolves net-bullish in the source itself (Zacks Rank #2 Buy, forward P/E ~25x below sector). No fundamental-deterioration story is present in the tape; the only bear thread is capex-digestion/underutilization risk if AI spending normalizes.
sentiment_narrative: Sentiment is strongly bullish with a vocal valuation-skeptic minority. Bull: analyst community overwhelmingly Buy/Strong Buy with rising targets; AI supercycle framed as multi-year and structural; the stock is +104.7% over 1y and +35.9% YTD, at/near 52-week highs ($432 vs $450 high). Bear/skeptic: GuruFocus GF Value flags the stock ~43% 'overvalued' on a 36.7x trailing P/E vs a 22.8x 5-yr median; one Seeking Alpha piece ('The AI Boom Is Coming To An End') argues capex/fixed-cost risk; insiders net SOLD (~$14M sold vs $1.2M bought over 3 months per GuruFocus) -- a soft negative, though insider ownership is tiny (0.04%) so the signal is weak. The stocktwits social feed in the bundle errored (HTTPError) -- retail social sentiment UNVERIFIED, flag accordingly. Net: the market BELIEVES the demand; the contest is over what multiple it deserves.

runway.q2_2026_guidance: Revenue $39.0-40.2B (+10% q/q, +32% y/y at midpoint); gross margin 65.5-67.5%; operating margin 56.5-58.5% (Q1 2026 call, Quartr eventId 541404).

monthly_sales_trend.read: Monthly sales remain in 30% YoY territory and accelerated sequentially in May; April's softer 17.5% y/y was a tough comp/timing artifact (a Bloomberg piece flagged 'slowest growth in months'), but the 5-month cumulative is +30% y/y, fully consistent with the >30% full-year USD guide. No deceleration signal yet.
monthly_sales_trend.source: pr.tsmc.com monthly revenue releases; Grafa/Bloomberg/TechTimes, May-Jun 2026

runway.capex_trajectory: FY2025 capex ~$40.6B (TWD 1,282.6B). FY2026 guided to the HIGH END of $52-56B (so ~$55-56B), up ~35% y/y. Q1 2026 capex was US$11.10B. Management states revenue growth will outpace capex growth, holding capital intensity healthy. Rising capex is a confidence signal here, but it also raises the fixed-cost-underutilization stakes if AI demand normalizes (the bear's lever).
runway.leading_edge_mix: Advanced nodes (7nm and below) = 74% of wafer revenue (3nm 25%, 5nm 36%, 7nm 13%). HPC platform = 61% of total revenue (+20% q/q). N2 in high-volume manufacturing since Q4 2025; N3 expanding in Taiwan, Arizona, Japan; A14 targeted for 2028 volume. The mix shift toward leading-edge + AI is the structural margin and growth engine.
runway.balance_sheet: Cash & marketable securities US$106B at Q1 2026 quarter-end; net cash position. The corrupted bundle net_debt/EV fields must be ignored; TSM is net cash.

runway_verdict: FUNDAMENTAL RUNWAY: STRONG and accelerating -- beat-and-raise print, >30% USD growth guide, 54-56% AI-accelerator CAGR to 2029, sold-out leading-edge capacity, monthly sales holding ~30% y/y, capex lifted to the high end on visible demand. VALUATION RISK (called out separately): the stock is +105% in a year, near 52-week highs, at ~27x FY2026e / 22x FY2027e EPS and ~37x trailing -- priced for the demand to CONTINUE. The two are distinct: a strong runway on a priced-for-perfection multiple. The downside is a de-rating on any AI-capex digestion or a Q2 print that merely meets (a 'beat-but-no-raise' would hit the multiple, not the fundamentals), plus margin dilution of 2-3% from the 2nm ramp and overseas fabs in 2026, and Middle East-tension input-cost risk flagged by management.

market_lean.strength: strong, but with a real valuation overhang
market_lean.rationale: The live evidence -- beat-and-raise, raised capex, 30% monthly sales, sold-out capacity, a wall of analyst raises with zero sells, $473 mean / $700 high targets -- leans clearly bullish on fundamentals. The single material counterweight is valuation (priced for continuation, +105% in 12m, vocal bubble debate, mild insider selling). This is CONTEXT for the panel, not a vote: the crux for Daedalus/Theia is duration -- how long the AI-accelerator CAGR and leading-edge scarcity persist before fading -- because the multiple already embeds multi-year continuation. The fundamental and valuation risks must be kept separate in the synthesis.

capex_guidance.buyer: TSMC (own capex, supply-side signal for Theia's demand curve)
